title: narcissus elegans subsp. intermedius
intro: Narcissus elegans subsp. intermedius, also known as the intermediate Spanish narcissus, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae, native to the Iberian Peninsula and southwestern France. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a purgative and to treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis. The health benefits of Narcissus elegans subsp. intermedius include its potential to reduce inflammation and to act as an antiseptic, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into tinctures, infusions, or salves, and are often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
